heaviest_rainfall.status: 20.31mm of rain was recorded as having fallen today, marking an exceptional precipitation event and it ranked 6th out of our records.  A rate of 5.4mm per hour was recorded during the 21.8 hour long storm.  The most intense rainfall recorded took place on 09 November 2025, with precipitation totalling 47.89mm.  The rain fell at 24.6mm per hour at times over the course of the 144.8 hour storm.

heaviest_rainrate.status: The heaviest rain rate registered stands at 34.3mm per hour on 02 September 2025, highlighting an extreme precipitation event.  21.19mm fell during the storm over 15.0 hours.

longest_rain.status: A relentless rainstorm took place on 09 November 2025, it lasted 144.8 hours and it delivered 47.89mm of rain at a maximum rate of 24.6mm per hour.

most_lightning.status: Since 11 April 2025, the day with the most lightning strikes is 07 September 2025 when 23 were seen.

storm.status: A storm is passing through the area.  This marks the first storm since 29 November 2025.

top_10_dry.status: The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 19 days.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 30 November 2024 and lasted for 10 days.

cold_snaps.status: At the moment, it is not a cold snap.  This year has seen 6 cold snaps; typically, 3.7 cold snaps happen every year.

extreme.status: The maximum temperature difference seen is 26.7°C, observed on 29 April 2025 when the temperature fell to 0.4°C but also managed to reach 27.1°C on the same day.

heatwaves.status: There is not an active heatwaves.  There has been 6 so far this year, while the average is 1.9.

tropical.status: The previous tropical night was on 18 July 2025, with only 62 in total since 1973.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is southerly (199°).  In the UK, a southerly wind usually brings milder, moist air from the Atlantic.

gusts.status: The record wind gust remains at 93.7 mph from 13 October 2012.

run.status: To date, the maximum wind run recorded is 1737 miles on 21 October 2012.

speed.status: The largest wind speed ever was on 13 October 2012 with a value of 93.7 mph.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, a bit warmer for the feels like temperature (up 6.9°C), outside temperature (up 6.2°C), and inside temperature (up 1.8°C) and more moisture-laden outside humidity (up 8%) and rainfall (up 1.9 mm).

Compared to today, yesterday also had noticeable differences in cloud levels (up 497 m).

The past week and month had observable variation in rainfall (down 1.5 mm), cloud levels (up 322 m), feels like temperature (down 2.5°C), and outside temperature (down 2.4°C) when compared to today.

Today’s average cloud levels is 496.8m lower than yesterday, 268.5m lower than the past week, 322.1m lower than the past month.

The cloud levels for yesterday ranged from 854m to 1568m and averaged 619.1m, while the past week and month indicated warmer conditions.

summary.summary: Inside; temperatures should be quite comfortable. Outside; cold weather is expected. Also, neither too dry nor too humid and near calm conditions. Some rain can be expected, overcast skies with low cloud, and light levels will be low during the day. The weather may change rapidly and become worse.

washing.phrase: The current weather conditions are not suitable as it is raining.
